steps.title: Find the missing width
steps.content:
The base area is length times width. With base area $35p$ and length $15$,
$$15w=35p$$
$$w=\frac{35p}{15}=\frac{7p}{3}$$

steps.title: Add the face areas
steps.content:
For a rectangular prism, surface area equals twice the sum of the three distinct face products:
$$S=2(lw+lh+wh)$$
Substitute $l=15$, $w=\frac{7p}{3}$, and $h=5$:
$$S=2(15\cdot\frac{7p}{3}+15\cdot5+\frac{7p}{3}\cdot5)$$

steps.title: Simplify the surface area
steps.content: Evaluate inside the parentheses, then combine the like terms with $p$: $$S=2(35p+75+\frac{35p}{3})$$ $$S=70p+150+\frac{70p}{3}$$ $$S=\frac{280p}{3}+150$$ The answer is B.
